evidence: `src/features/gallery/hooks/useAdjacentPagePreloader.ts` is framed as a unified preloading boundary but imports both data-fetch shape (`fetchGenerations`) and orchestration runtime (`scheduleAdjacentPagePreload`), coupling one hook to multiple subsystem ownership areas.

`src/features/gallery/hooks/useAdjacentPagePreloader.ts` derives `GenerationFilters` from `Parameters<typeof fetchGenerations>[3]`, creating a hidden positional contract across module boundaries instead of a named domain contract.

Overlay/modal ownership is split across many UI files (`src/shared/components/ModalContainer.tsx`, `src/pages/Home/components/InstallInstructionsModal.tsx`, `src/shared/components/LoraSelectorModal/components/DescriptionModal.tsx`, `src/shared/components/MediaTypeFilter.tsx`, `src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem/components/InfoTooltip.tsx`, `src/pages/Home/components/CreativePartnerPane.tsx`) with inconsistent state responsibilities (some pass `OverlayVisibilityState`, others pass multiple setter props).

Several high-import hub files in this batch are intentionally thin facades (`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts`, `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts`, `src/shared/components/ui/cn.ts`, `src/shared/hooks/mobile/index.ts`), which helps API stability but concentrates change risk into central adapter points.

src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts defines both `TravelPayloadCore` and `TravelBetweenImagesOrchestratorPayload` with near-duplicate field inventories, then also emits `task_payload_contract`, creating triple-represented schema intent.

src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts similarly layers `JoinClipsBaseOrchestratorDetails`/union modes plus `JoinClipsTaskPayload` and `task_payload_contract`, so contract abstractions are additive rather than simplifying.

Task builders across src/shared/lib/tasks/imageGeneration.ts, src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts, and src/shared/lib/tasks/magicEdit.ts repeat validation + payload mapping scaffolds with only small task-specific deltas.
src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx constructs a large environment object and forwards many grouped props/hooks, making seam contracts broad and implicit.

src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx assembles multiple slice models (`core`, `media`, `variants`, `navigation`) and passes them into layout/action workflows, coupling many collaborators at a single integration point.

src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/layouts/LightboxLayout.tsx consumes many contexts and model picks, then fans state into many child controls, increasing cross-module handoff surface area.

src/shared/lib/taskParamsUtils.ts contains deep fallback chains (`deriveInputImages`, `derivePrompt`) across legacy and contract fields; precedence is encoded as long conditional sequences rather than explicit strategy objects.

src/shared/components/ImageGenerationDetails.tsx and src/shared/components/TaskDetails/VideoTravelDetails.tsx still perform additional local fallback/mapping logic on top of task view models, so parsing/display responsibilities remain partially duplicated.

src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useSegmentSlotMode.ts has large mixed-purpose internals (URL state parsing, reducer state, structure-video overlap resolution, deep-link handling) within one hook boundary.

findings.summary: The gallery preloader hook mixes feature orchestration with shared data-contract inference, weakening subsystem ownership boundaries.
evidence: `useAdjacentPagePreloader` imports `fetchGenerations` only to infer filter type via `Parameters<typeof fetchGenerations>[3]`, coupling this feature hook to another module's function signature.
The same hook also imports `scheduleAdjacentPagePreload`, so data contract and orchestration policy are both anchored in one feature entry point.
findings.suggestion: Promote a named `GenerationFilters` contract to a shared/domain type module and inject preloading orchestration behind a small feature-facing interface so the hook depends on stable contracts rather than positional signatures.

findings.summary: Overlay and modal visibility ownership is distributed inconsistently across page and shared UI layers.
evidence: `ModalContainer` requires external `open/onOpenChange`, but policy behavior (`preventOutsideClose`) is handled internally, splitting responsibility.
`InstallInstructionsModal` and `DescriptionModal` pass through overlay state directly, while `CreativePartnerPane` manages extra tooltip open/disabled flags and passes multiple setters.
`MediaTypeFilter` and `InfoTooltip` each adapt optional visibility props through local policy hooks, indicating duplicated boundary decisions at component level.
findings.suggestion: Standardize one overlay ownership pattern per UI layer (for example: container-owned policy hooks + stateless presentational components) and move tooltip/modal policy into dedicated controllers to reduce page-level state choreography.

findings.summary: Task payload abstractions duplicate the same schema across core interfaces, payload interfaces, and embedded contracts.
evidence: `TravelPayloadCore` and `TravelBetweenImagesOrchestratorPayload` repeat nearly all payload fields; `buildTravelBetweenImagesPayload` then adds `task_payload_contract` and spreads payload core anyway.
`JoinClipsTaskPayload` repeats explicit top-level fields while also storing `task_payload_contract` and mode-specific orchestrator details, producing multiple sources of truth.
Individual task creators still manually shape payload objects even where contract builders exist, indicating abstractions are not reducing mapping burden.
findings.suggestion: Define one authoritative orchestrator-details shape per task family and generate transport payloads from it (or vice versa), with contract metadata attached once. Remove parallel interface inventories and rely on shared contract-to-payload adapters.

findings.summary: Lightbox integration seams are overly broad, causing high coupling between orchestration hooks and layout components.
evidence: Image and video lightbox paths create large environment/state composites and propagate them through multiple orchestration hooks.
Render-model assembly in `useVideoLightboxRenderModel` aggregates many concerns (state slices, workflow props, actions, accessibility, panel generation) in one handoff layer.
`LightboxLayout` then consumes many context slices and conditionals for media/edit/navigation/tool overlays, indicating integration glue concentration.
findings.suggestion: Split lightbox seams into smaller contracts (navigation, media display, edit tools, panel content) with explicit DTOs per concern; keep orchestration hooks thin and move conditional UI branching into dedicated presenters.

findings.summary: Task param interpretation relies on long fallback chains and mixed responsibilities, reducing local clarity and predictability.
evidence: `deriveInputImages` and `derivePrompt` branch across many legacy/current keys (`task_view_contract`, nested orchestrator payloads, segment params, top-level fields), making precedence hard to reason about.
Detail components still contain additional fallback/formatting branches after calling shared view-model builders, implying the parser contract is not sufficiently complete.
Runtime behavior depends on optional `Record<string, unknown>` probing rather than stronger typed discriminants for task parameter versions.
findings.suggestion: Introduce versioned task-param adapters with explicit discriminated shapes (`contract_v1`, `legacy_v0`, etc.), centralize precedence policy there, and make detail components consume normalized typed view models only.
